title: network flag accepts arbitrary string, creates permanent garbage wallet.json
description:
Scenario: user runs 'nie --network Mainnet wallet init' (capital M) or '--network testnet2'. The value is stored verbatim in wallet.json. Every subsequent command using --network mainnet (the default) then fails: 'This wallet was created for Mainnet. Use --network Mainnet or create a new mainnet wallet.' User cannot escape without manually editing wallet.json or deleting it — neither is documented.

Maintenance consequence: will generate issue reports from users who fat-fingered the flag. Fix is a one-liner in clap:
  #[arg(long, global = true, value_parser = ["mainnet", "testnet"], default_value = "mainnet")]
This converts a future-me maintenance headache into 'error: invalid value' at parse time.
